If you NOTICE a problem OUTSIDE this slice's scope (a flaky test, a latent bug, a

included, may add to it. Writing such a NOTE is the one exception to the "no file
changes outside your slice" rule below — it is a note, not work.)

move any files between work/ folders, and do not touch work/in-progress/complete-from-needs-attention.md.
The runner (or human) owns every git-state transition. (Your TESTS may freely
create and operate on their OWN throwaway git repos — that is expected.)

When the acceptance criteria are met and the repo's build/test/format checks are
green, STOP and report what you did. The runner handles the `git mv` to
work/done/, the completion commit, and integration.

### Claiming this slice

```sh
I'll start by reading the brief and the key context files.

---
title: complete a good needs-attention item — runner-owned needs-attention → done (re-gate, no manual git)

## What to build

> Self-contained feature \u2014 derives from NO PRD (`covers: []`), so per
> WORK-CONTRACT.md it omits `prd:` and is its own source of truth. Spotted live (3
> dogfood runs in): recovering a good-but-stuck item required raw git. Delete
> `work/observations/needs-attention-recovery-needs-runner-owned-complete.md` once
> this lands.

A **runner-owned `needs-attention → done` path**: when a `do`/`run` gate failure was
SPURIOUS (the work is actually fine \u2014 e.g. an env-polluted test, a transient flake,
or a cause the human has since fixed), let the runner re-gate the item's branch and,
if green, COMPLETE it \u2014 so the human never does git. Pairs with `requeue`
(`needs-attention → backlog`, the give-up/redo path); this is the "it's fine, finish
it" path.

### Mechanism (DECIDED \u2014 extend `complete`, no new verb, no `resume` dependency)

`complete` (`src/complete.ts`) already does the whole ritual (gate \u2192 done-move \u2192
rebase \u2192 integrate). Its ONLY blocker is that it requires the item in
`work/in-progress/` (the `existsSync(inProgress)` guard at ~L273 refuses otherwise:
"nothing to complete"). So:

- **Make `complete` ALSO accept a `needs-attention/` item.** When
  `work/in-progress/<slug>.md` is absent, fall back to
  `work/needs-attention/<slug>.md`; if found there, treat it as the recovery source.
- **Re-run the GATE (authoritative \u2014 do NOT trust the human blindly).** The recovery
  only completes if the gate is now GREEN. A genuinely-bad item cannot be
  force-completed through this path (the existing human-only `--skip-verify` escape
  hatch remains the deliberate, loud override; the runner still never skips).
- **On green, do the `needs-attention → done` transition** (instead of
  `in-progress → done`): `git mv work/needs-attention/<slug>.md →
  work/done/<slug>.md`, commit, rebase, integrate \u2014 the same runner-owned machinery,
  just a different source folder. The recorded `## Needs attention` reason block in
  the body: leave it as durable history (do NOT silently scrub it) unless trivial to
  decide otherwise \u2014 a completed item keeping its "was stuck because X" note is fine.
- **RECONCILE the on-`main` surfacing commit.** The needs-attention move was
  cherry-picked to `<arbiter>/main` (the autonomous on-`main` surfacing). Completing
  the item must reconcile that so the human does not hit a rebase conflict against
  the surfacing commit (the manual recovery hit EXACTLY this). The runner-owned path
  must handle the reconciliation (the done-move supersedes the surfaced
  needs-attention state on `main`).

### Scope + non-dependencies

- **Do NOT depend on `resume`** \u2014 `human-face-verbs` (which adds `resume`) is NOT
  built yet. This is a `complete` extension, standalone.
- Applies to the in-place `complete` (human) AND is what `do`'s autonomous path uses
  on a retry; keep it consistent with `complete`'s existing merge/propose +
  surfacing semantics.
- This is RECOVERY of an already-claimed item \u2014 NOT a new claim. The item is already
  the operator's (it is in their `needs-attention/`); completing it needs no re-claim.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `complete <slug>` (and the equivalent path `do` uses on retry) accepts a slug
      whose file is in `work/needs-attention/` (falling back when `in-progress/` is
      absent), RE-RUNS the gate, and on GREEN does the `needs-attention → done`
      move + commit + rebase + integrate \u2014 with no manual git from the human.
- [ ] If the re-gate is RED, it does NOT complete (the item stays in
      needs-attention/); the gate stays authoritative (`--skip-verify` remains the
      only, human-only, loud override).
- [ ] The cherry-picked on-`main` surfacing of the needs-attention move is
      reconciled by completion (no leftover/conflicting surfaced state; the
      done-move supersedes it) \u2014 the human does not hit a rebase conflict.
- [ ] The pre-existing `in-progress → done` completion is UNCHANGED (no regression);
      only a `needs-attention/` SOURCE fallback + the surfacing reconciliation are
      added.
- [ ] Tests (throwaway repos + local `--bare` arbiter): a seeded needs-attention
      item with a now-green branch completes to done/ via `complete` (no manual git);
      a still-red one is refused and stays in needs-attention/; the normal
      in-progress completion still works; the surfacing commit is reconciled.
- [ ] `pnpm -r build && pnpm -r test && pnpm -r format:check` green.

## Blocked by

- `do-in-place` \u2014 the autonomous `do` path + its needs-attention surfacing (the
  arbiter-passed routing) shipped with the keystone (in `done/`); this recovers
  items that path produces and reuses `complete`'s machinery. Build on it.

> rebase + integrate (same machinery, different source folder). RECONCILE the
> cherry-picked on-`main` surfacing of the needs-attention move so the human does
> not hit a rebase conflict (the done-move supersedes the surfaced state).
>

> gate/done-move/rebase/integrate flow; the `surfaceArbiter` + merge/propose
> semantics), `src/needs-attention.ts` (the `## Needs attention` reason block + the
> move/surfacing helpers; how the surfacing commit was made \u2014 to reconcile it),
> `src/ledger-write.ts` (the transition seam), and the observation above. Keep the
> in-progress→done path unchanged.
>

> needs-attention item with a now-green branch \u2192 `complete` lands it in done/ with
> no manual git; a still-red one \u2192 refused, stays in needs-attention/; the normal
> in-progress completion still works; the surfacing commit is reconciled. "Done" =
> acceptance criteria met and the gate green.

The key reconciliation insight, mirroring `publishSurfaceCommit`: rather than rely on `git rebase` replaying the historical needs-attention move commit (which conflicts against the surfaced `main`), I should make the work branch carry the needs-attention→done move and reconcile by **resolving the surface on main as part of completion** — i.e. the done-move on `main` supersedes the surfaced needs-attention placement.

Let me think about the most robust, minimal approach that reuses existing machinery:

**The recovery flow:**
1. Detect source = `work/needs-attention/<slug>.md` (fallback when in-progress absent).
2. Re-run gate. Red → refuse (stays in needs-attention).
3. Green → `git mv needs-attention → done`, commit agent work + move.
4. Rebase onto `arbiter/main` — **this conflicts** because the work branch's historical move-only commit (in-progress→needs-attention) plus our new needs-attention→done can't replay onto a main where the file is already in needs-attention.

The reconciliation: instead of a plain rebase, I'll **clear the needs-attention surface on main first** (publish a surface commit that moves needs-attention→in-progress on main, like `resolveFromNeedsAttention` does), THEN rebase. But that adds a commit to main racing with the done-move.

Actually the cleanest: the done-move itself, when published via the COMPLETE transition's integration, needs main's tree to be reconcilable. The simplest robust mechanism that exactly matches the existing `publishSurfaceCommit` reconcile-from-main's-own-state idea:

**Before the rebase, when recovering from needs-attention, REBASE the work branch onto arbiter/main but DROP the historical needs-attention move-only commit** — because main already reflects that move (the item is in needs-attention on main). Then our work branch just needs to carry: the agent's original work (wip) + the new needs-attention→done move, replayed onto a main that has the file in needs-attention/. The done-move `git mv needs-attention/<slug> → done/<slug>` will apply cleanly since main has it in needs-attention/.

Now I have full clarity. The work branch (`work/<slug>`) tip after a `do` gate failure: `base...wip-commit...move-only-commit(in-progress→needs-attention)`. The arbiter's `main` was surfaced (item reproduced into `needs-attention/` on main from main's own state). The work branch is pushed to `arbiter/work/<slug>`.

So when the human/runner runs `complete <slug>` to recover:
- Working tree: on `work/<slug>`, item at `work/needs-attention/<slug>.md` (because the move-only commit put it there).
- Re-gate (green).
- Need to: `needs-attention → done` move + commit + rebase + integrate, reconciling the main surface.

**The reconciliation strategy** (matching the codebase's "reproduce from main's own state" philosophy): Since the work branch's history contains the in-progress→needs-attention move-only commit, and main ALSO has the item in needs-attention/, a plain rebase of `needs-attention→done` onto main will replay the historical move-only commit which conflicts. 

The clean solution: when recovering from needs-attention, **rebase with `--onto` to drop the historical needs-attention move commit**, so only the wip + the new done-move replay onto main. Then the done-move (`needs-attention → done`) applies cleanly onto main (which has it in needs-attention/), and the integration's CAS supersedes the surfaced state — no conflict for the human.

+772 /**
+773  * The RECOVERY rebase: rebase the work branch onto `<arbiter>/main` while
+774  * DROPPING the historical `in-progress → needs-attention` move-only commit, so
+775  * the replay does not conflict with the surfaced needs-attention state already
+776  * on `main`.
+777  *
+778  * The work branch (as the autonomous `do`/`run` path left it) carries, ABOVE the
+779  * claim-time main: a `wip` commit (the aborted agent work), the route-to-
+780  * needs-attention `chore(<slug>): route to needs-attention; …` MOVE-ONLY commit,
+781  * and (just committed by `complete`) the `needs-attention → done` done-move. The
+782  * arbiter's `main` was surfaced to ALSO hold the item in needs-attention/, so a
+783  * plain rebase replays the historical move-only commit (in-progress → needs-
+784  * attention) onto a main that has no in-progress/<slug>.md → conflict.
+785  *
+786  * We rebase the whole range `(merge-base, HEAD]` `--onto <arbiter>/main` and use
+787  * a `GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR` that DELETES the move-only commit's line from the todo
+788  * list (matched by its message). The remaining `wip + (needs-attention → done)`
+789  * replays cleanly: wip touches only the agent's files, and the done-move's
+790  * `git mv work/needs-attention/<slug>.md → work/done/<slug>.md` applies because
+791  * the surfaced main HAS the item in needs-attention/. Result: the done-move
+792  * supersedes the surfaced state, and the human never sees a conflict.
+793  *
+794  * When the branch carries NO such move-only commit (e.g. a needs-attention item
+795  * placed by hand, never autonomously surfaced), the editor deletes nothing and
+796  * this degrades to a normal rebase. The seam returns the rebase RunResult so the
+797  * caller's existing conflict-abort path handles a genuine (non-surface) conflict
+798  * unchanged.
+799  */

## Summary

I implemented the runner-owned **`needs-attention → done` recovery path** by extending `complete` (no new verb, no `resume` dependency), satisfying every acceptance criterion.

### What I changed — `packages/agent-runner/src/complete.ts`

1. **Source-folder fallback (the guard at ~L273).** The "must be in-progress" guard now prefers `work/in-progress/<slug>.md` but **falls back to `work/needs-attention/<slug>.md`** when in-progress is absent. It refuses only when *neither* folder holds the item (message updated accordingly). A `recovering` flag records that we came from needs-attention.

2. **Re-gate stays authoritative.** On the recovery path a **red re-gate does NOT complete** — the item simply stays in `needs-attention/` (no re-route, no re-surface, no duplicate reason block). `--skip-verify` remains the only, human-only, loud override. The pre-existing in-progress red-gate routing is untouched.

3. **Done-move from the resolved source.** `git mv work/<source>/<slug>.md → work/done/<slug>.md` (in-progress on the normal path, needs-attention on recovery) + the same atomic commit.

4. **Surfacing reconciliation (the rebase).** The new `rebaseDroppingNeedsAttentionSurface` helper does an interactive `rebase --onto <arbiter>/main <merge-base>` that **drops the historical `in-progress → needs-attention` move-only commit** (via a slug-anchored `GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR` sed). The replay becomes `wip + (needs-attention → done)`, which applies cleanly onto the *surfaced* main (which already has the item in needs-attention/) — so the **done-move supersedes the surfaced state and the human never hits a rebase conflict**. It passes the branch *name* (not `HEAD`) so the branch ref is updated, not detached. The in-progress→done path is unchanged (plain rebase).

### Tests — `packages/agent-runner/test/complete-from-needs-attention.test.ts` (8, all green)
Throwaway repos + local `--bare` arbiter, using the real `applyNeedsAttentionTransition` to produce the genuine surfaced state: green re-gate lands it in done/ (merge + propose) with no manual git; a fresh human clone fast-forwards with **no conflict**; still-red is refused and stays in needs-attention/ (locally and surfaced); `--skip-verify` overrides; the reason block survives as durable history; and the normal in-progress→done completion + the "neither folder" refusal both still work.

### Gate
`pnpm -r build && pnpm -r test && pnpm -r format:check` — **green** (596 tests pass).
